headline: Quant funds hit worst momentum rout of the year
detail: When quant/momentum funds blow up, they systematically dump their biggest winners - which are almost always high-flying tech and chip names. Your entire book (CRWD, SOXL, SMH, QQQ, CRWD scanner setup) sits squarely in the blast zone. This kind of selling can be indiscriminate and fast, especially on margin.
rec: No new adds into your tech/chip names until this momentum unwind shows signs of stabilizing. Watch SMH and QQQ closely - if either breaks intraday support levels, consider trimming SOXL first given its leverage.

headline: Digital Realty falls 4% after $3.5B Blackstone data center deal
detail: Data center and cloud infrastructure stocks getting hit on deal-related dilution concerns is a secondary negative read-through for cloud-adjacent names. It doesn't hit your holdings directly but adds to the cautious tone around the broader tech trade today.
rec: No action required in your current holdings, but if you're watching DDOG or NET from the scanner list, this adds one more reason not to chase entries today - wait for cleaner conditions.

headline: Inflation likely to stay significantly above target, warns top European central banker
detail: Sticky inflation signals that rates-higher-for-longer is not just a US story. That's a valuation headwind for growth and tech stocks globally, since their valuations are most sensitive to discount rates. Your concentrated tech/margin book is the most exposed type of portfolio to this dynamic.
rec: No immediate trade triggered, but this reinforces avoiding adding new leveraged or speculative positions (SOXL, SPCX) today. Hold your stops and let this macro noise clear before sizing up anywhere.

headline: Strait of Hormuz reopening faster than expected, Morgan Stanley cuts oil target
detail: Falling oil prices are broadly a mild positive for the economy and can reduce inflation pressure slightly, which is a small indirect positive for tech valuations. However, the effect on your specific chip and tech names is minimal and indirect - not a driver today.
rec: This does not give a real reason to add or trim anything in your book. The inflation headline above is the more relevant macro signal for your names today.
